data.title: Cal-Maine Foods: All Outstanding Shrs of Class A Common Stk Converted Into Shrs of Common Stk >CALM
South Korea Import Prices Rise for Fifth Month
data.description:
South Korea's import prices rose 3.4% from a year earlier in March 2025, easing from a downwardly revised 4.3% increase in the previous month.

It marked the fifth straight month of rising import costs, largely driven by a weaker Korean won.

Prices increased for consumer goods (5.8% vs. 4.5%), intermediate goods (6.1% vs. 5.9%), and capital goods (6.9% vs. 5.2%), while raw material prices declined by 2.5%, reversing a 1.8% gain in the prior month.

On a monthly basis, import prices dipped 0.4% in March, following an upwardly revised 1% drop in February.

data.title: CleanSpark to start selling Bitcoin in 'self-funding' pivot
data.description:
CleanSpark will start selling a portion of the Bitcoin earned from its mining operations each month in a bid to become financially self-sufficient, the US Bitcoin miner said on April 15. 

In addition, CleanSpark secured a $200 million credit facility backed by Bitcoin through an agreement with Coinbase Prime, the institutional brokerage division of the crypto exchange, according to a statement.

Together, the Bitcoin sales and credit line mean CleanSpark has “achieved escape velocity — the ability to self-fund operations, augment our bitcoin treasury, and contribute to expansion capital through operational cash flow,” Zach Bradford, CEO of CleanSpark, said. 

CleanSpark has opened an institutional Bitcoin trading desk to facilitate the cryptocurrency sales, it added. 

Navigating market volatility

The Bitcoin miner’s emphasis on self-funding comes as mining stocks reel from across-the-board selloffs in the first quarter of 2025. 

Shares of CoinShares Crypto Miners ETF (WGMI) — a publicly traded fund tracking a diverse basket of Bitcoin mining stocks — are down more than 40% since the start of the year, according to data from Morningstar. 

“[W]e believe this is the right time to evolve from a nearly 100% hold strategy adopted in mid-2023 and move back using a portion of our monthly production to support operations,” Bradford said. 

Cheaper stock prices effectively increase Bitcoin miners’ cost of capital and can potentially cause creditors to demand faster loan repayments. 

Analysts at JP Morgan attributed the downturn to eroding cryptocurrency prices, which added pressure to business models already strained by the Bitcoin network’s April 2024 halving. 

Halvings occur roughly every four years when the Bitcoin network automatically cuts mining rewards in half. 

In April, pressure on mining stocks worsened when US President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs on US imports.

US Bitcoin miners are especially vulnerable to trade wars because they rely on specialized mining hardware, often sourced from foreign manufacturers.

Bradford said he expects CleanSpark’s financial self-sufficiency to differentiate it from peers “who continue to rely on equity dilution to fund operating costs or increased leverage to grow their Bitcoin reserves.”

Other miners are taking similarly aggressive measures to adapt to the changing market.

Bitdeer, a Singapore-based crypto miner, has reportedly touted plans to start manufacturing mining hardware in the United States to mitigate the impact of Trump’s planned import tariffs.

data.title: Skyward Specialty suffers data breach with documents appearing on dark web
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Skyward Specialty Insurance Group has written to customers and regulators that it suffered a data breach with certain documents having been made accessible on the dark web.

Houston, Texas-based Skyward Specialty explained in a letter sent to clients and state regulators dated April 8 that it was recently alerted that a database where it stored certain documents was publicly discoverable.

“Skyward Specialty determined that this was made possible due to a miscalibration in the database settings,” the company said.

While the carrier confirmed it has remediated that miscalibration, on April 5 it was alerted that certain documents that had been publicly discoverable in the database had been made accessible on the dark web.

“Our investigation into the incident is ongoing and will provide updates when relevant information is available,” the Andrew Robinson-led carrier said.

It is unclear precisely what information was compromised, and how many clients are affected.

According to the National Association of Attorneys General, data breaches can be defined “as the unlawful and unauthorized acquisition of personal information that compromises the security, confidentiality, or integrity of personal information."

What is regarded personal information depends on individual state law.

However, such information usually includes an individual’s first and last name along with at least one or more of that person’s social security number; driver’s license number or state-issued ID card number; and a bank account number, credit or debit card number, combined with any security code, access code, PIN or password needed to access an account.

It could also include medical history or health information, biometric information, email address and password or tax ID number.

In the letter, titled Notification of Potential Data Incident, Skyward Specialty said that “pursuant to its requirements under applicable insurance sector regulations," the company had written regarding a potential data incident that it had recently become aware of.

“While Skyward Specialty is notifying you of this incident, Skyward Specialty does not waive any rights or defenses related to the incident,” the company said.

At the time of publication, Skyward Specialty had not responded to a request for comment.

data.title: Price Impact From Car Tariffs Could Be Muted, J.D. Power Says
data.description:
By Mike Colias

Car-price increases might not be as severe as many analysts predict even if the bevy of tariffs now hitting the auto industry sticks, new research from J.D. Power found.

The firm projects an average price increase by the end of this year of roughly $2,300 per vehicle, a rise of about 5%. That is significantly below some forecasts of U.S. car buyers paying $10,000 or even $20,000 more on some models because of tariffs.

The firm says higher costs from tariffs will affect carmakers unevenly, making it difficult for harder-hit brands to pass along extra expenses to car buyers. Some brands might see their costs go up only 5% or 10%, whereas others face a 25% spike.

"If anyone tries to pass through tariffs at an elevated level, let's call it 10 or 15 percentage points more than their competitors, they will face very large volume declines," Thomas King, J.D. Power's chief product officer, said at a conference Tuesday ahead of the New York auto show this week.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Tuesday at 5 PM ET
data.description:
U.S. Stocks Edge Lower While Investors Wait for Signs on Tariff Talks

The Dow Industrials and the S&P 500 slid fractionally; bank stocks gained on strong earnings reports.

Big Banks Alarmed After Their Regulator Gets Hacked

JPMorgan and others have curbed some electronic information-sharing with the OCC.

Canada's Carney Warns of Further Auto Fallout With Trump 'Attacking' Domestic Sector

Prime Minister Mark Carney said he and other senior officials have spoken with senior executives at the world's biggest automakers.

China's Port Cargos Start to Slow as Tariff Tensions Escalate

Chinese port activity slumped in early April, with data indicating that exports to the U.S. have been hammered by President Trump's tariffs.

U.S. Import Prices Slide Ahead of New Tariffs

Lower prices for fuel imports offset higher prices or nonfuel imports last month.

Canada Inflation Cools to 2.3% in March

Still, the consumer-price index topped the Bank of Canada's 2% target for a second straight month.

The Dollar Has Been Tumbling. It May Have Further to Fall.

An unusual dynamic has led some to worry that U.S. investments are losing their appeal as a global safe haven.

German Economic Sentiment Collapses on Trump's Tariff Chaos

The outlook for the German economy cratered this month after Trump's tariff blitz, according to a monthly survey of financial analysts.

Forecasts Tilt Toward Rate Pause From Bank of Canada This Week

By a narrow margin, economists expect the central bank to take a wait-and-see approach at its policy meeting Wednesday, given concerns over tariff-fueled inflation.

IEA Cuts Oil Demand Forecast as U.S. Tariffs Weigh on Economic Growth

Oil demand growth is forecast to be weaker than previously projected as escalating global trade tensions sparked by U.S. President Trump's tariffs weigh on the global economy, the International Energy Agency said.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Tuesday at 5 PM ET
data.description:
United Airlines Tops Expectations Despite Travel Demand Worries

The airline posted adjusted per-share earnings of 91 cents, ahead of the 74 cents forecast by analysts.

Big Banks Show Consumers Remained Resilient Heading Into Tariff Turmoil

Bank of America and Citigroup said consumer spending ticked higher in the first quarter, when concerns about the economy began to bubble up.

Zuckerberg, on Witness Stand, Rebuffs Claims Meta Monopolizes Social Media Market

The Meta Platforms CEO testified that his company had legitimate business reasons for acquiring Instagram more than a decade ago.

Boeing Shares Rebound From Loss After China Cuts Off New Orders

Over the past five years, China has already cut Boeing deliveries by 84% compared with the five years before.

Citigroup Reports Jump in Profit, Revenue

Citigroup reported a jump in profit and revenue in the first quarter of 2025 that beat analysts' expectations, thanks in part to market volatility that generated higher fees for its trading business.

J&J Boosts Outlook Despite Incoming Tariff Costs

New tariffs, primarily on medical technology products, are projected to add $400 million in costs this year, Chief Financial Officer Joseph Wolk said.

Albertsons Sales Grow on Boost From Pharmacy Business

Albertsons saw its top-line figure increase in the latest quarter as its pharmacy business powered identical sales growth.

Blackstone Moves to Extend Its Reach Into Everyday Investors' Portfolios

The firm is joining with Vanguard and Wellington to offer portfolios with public and private assets.

American Airlines to Provide Free Wi-Fi, Joining Race to Court Connected Travelers

The deal with AT&T will provide free in-flight internet to loyalty program members, beginning in January.

Trump Gave Automakers a Tariff Break. It's Causing More Confusion.

Industry executives are stumped on how to claim the U.S.-content deduction, leaving in limbo billions of dollars in potential savings.

data.title: Materials Shares Drop TuesdayMaterials Roundup
data.description:
Shares in the materials sector slid as global markets took a break from the sharp swings that marked trading since President Trump announced his tariffs package on Apr. 2.

Bank of America downgraded Dow Inc. to underperform from buy, saying the chemical company faces significant earnings headwinds from an economic slowdown and intensifying trade war that could put its rich dividend at risk.

In a note, BofA said Dow faces a "perfect storm" of softening macro, emerging barriers to trade and higher U.S. feedstock costs, and the analysts slash their 2025 and 2026 Ebitda estimates by 17% and 23%, respectively.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Energy Shares Fell Slightly Tuesday in Calmer TradeEnergy Roundup
data.description:
Energy stocks were slightly lower as global markets traded sideways amid a lack of major developments in U.S. tariffs that drove investment in recent weeks.

Oil futures tread water for much of the session and settled lower as markets appeared to steady after two weeks of tariff-driven volatility.

Future demand remains at the forefront of trader concerns, with the IEA cutting its 2025 forecast to around 730,000 barrels a day from just over 1 million b/d, following demand downgrades by OPEC and the U.S. EIA.

Meanwhile, the Keystone pipeline that carries Canadian oil to the U.S. Midwest restarted following repairs after last week's spill, the Pipeline Safety and Hazardous Materials Administration said.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Cal-Maine Foods: Intends to Purchase Up to $50M of Shrs of Common Stk Being Sold From Selling Stockholders >CALM
NET Power Names Marc Horstman as Chief Operating Officer
data.description:
NET Power said late Tuesday it appointed Marc Horstman as chief operating officer, effective immediately.

Horstman was most recently the head of product development at the company and will succeed former COO and president Brian Allen, who will depart on May 1, the company said.

Chief Executive Danny Rice will assume the title of president and serve as the interim chief financial officer. Former CFO Akash Patel was relieved of his duties, effective May 1, the company said.

data.title: XWELL Reports Fiscal Year 2024 Results
data.description:
NEW YORK, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — XWELL, Inc. ("XWELL" or the "Company"), a pioneer in democratizing wellness, today reported results for the year ended December 31, 2024.

Recent Highlights:

  • XWELL delivered 2024 revenue growth of approximately 13% versus 2023.
  • Gross margin more than doubled, increasing from 12.2% in 2023 to 26.3% in 2024.
  • The Company reduced operating and overhead expenses in 2023 and 2024, while it continues to focus on returning to overall profitability. For the year ended December 31, 2024, the Company:
    • Reduced salaries and benefits by approximately 5% versus 2023.
    • Reduced general and administrative expenses by approximately 4% versus 2023.
    • Reduced total operating expenses by approximately 19% versus 2023, even with substantial non-recurring expenses incurred in 2024.
  • XWELL announced a three-year extension of its Traveler-based Genomic Surveillance Program in partnership with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, reinforcing its critical role in national biosecurity.
  • Accelerating its expansion outside of airport locations, XWELL launched a new Naples Wax Center in Estero, Florida in December 2024.
  • Strengthening its capital structure, XWELL successfully closed a $4 million private placement in January 2025, comprising of convertible preferred stock and warrants.
  • Leveraging its recent capital raise, XWELL announced plans to acquire select medical spas to expand into the high-growth wellness and beauty sector.
  • As part of its brand evolution, XWELL announced that it plans to unite all of its wellness offerings under a single, cohesive XWELL brand identity.

“We believe that XWELL’s improved 2024 financial and operational performance reflects the successful execution of our growth and productivity initiatives,” said Ezra Ernst, CEO of XWELL. “We continue to capitalize on compelling growth opportunities across our brands and remain focused on achieving sustainable expansion alongside our relentless focus on wellness and our customers.”

“We’re also grateful and proud to continue the work we developed with our partners at the CDC and Ginkgo Bioworks for another three years. An early warning system for dangerous pathogens, the Traveler-based Genomic Surveillance Program plays a crucial role in protecting national security and public health.” Mr. Ernst added, “Looking ahead, I’m excited about the opportunities ahead for XWELL. By accelerating growth both in and out of the airport, unifying our offerings under the XWELL brand, and reinforcing our role in U.S. biosecurity and exploring biosecurity opportunities outside of the United States, I believe that we’re positioning XWELL for continued growth and long-term value creation.”

Bringing A Unified Wellness Brand to the MarketCommitted to capitalizing on compelling growth opportunities in the wellness market, XWELL has developed and communicated a clear vision, mission, and purpose-driven forward-looking plan.

  • Our vision is to liberate wellness, making it a mainstream category synonymous with health, balance, and self-care.
  • Our mission is to create environments that inspire confidence, self-improvement, and wellness for everyone, everywhere.
  • Our purpose is to reshape the way people think about wellness by showing how accessible and effortless it can be.

The Company’s forward-looking plan focuses on expanding and integrating offerings across its brands, with a key emphasis on unifying airport and off-airport locations under the XWELL brand. This strategic alignment will enable the development of membership and loyalty programs like Priority Pass that provide seamless access to XWELL locations, fostering deeper customer relationships and enhancing brand loyalty. Additionally, a strong customer community will support targeted marketing initiatives and cross-promotional opportunities, strengthened by advanced technology and customer relationship management capabilities from the HyperPointe unit.

At the same time, XWELL is actively broadening its retail product portfolio to feature a range of cutting-edge wellness offerings. These offerings include state-of-the-art wellness devices, nutritional supplements, and innovative wellness patches — each designed to support holistic health and cater to the evolving needs of today’s wellness-conscious consumers.

Planned Strategic Investment in Medical SpasIn March 2025, XWELL unveiled plans to acquire select medical spas during 2025, leveraging its recent $4 million private placement to expand into the high-growth wellness and beauty sector.

This strategy aligns with XWELL’s mission to liberate wellness by creating a seamless continuum of care, extending beyond airports and into metropolitan areas where demand for advanced beauty and wellness treatments is rising.

XWELL will initially focus on select metropolitan areas with strong demand for medspa services, including Orlando, Austin, Texas, and Salt Lake City.

Operating At the Intersection of Travel, Health and Wellness

Operating at the intersection of travel, health and wellness, the Company’s brands currently include XWELL™, XpresSpa®, Treat™, Naples Wax Center®, XpresCheck® and HyperPointe™. 

Travel Wellness Portfolio - XpresSpa®XpresSpa is the leading airport retailer of wellness services and related retail offerings. As of December 31, 2024, there were 18 domestic XpresSpa locations in total, comprised of 17 Company-owned locations and one franchise. The Company also had 10 international locations operating as of December 31, 2024, including two XpresSpa locations in the Dubai International Airport in the United Arab Emirates, one XpresSpa location in the Zayad International Airport in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, three XpresSpa locations in the Schiphol Amsterdam Airport in the Netherlands and four XpresSpa locations in the Istanbul Airport in Turkey.

Out-of-Airport Wellness Portfolio - Naples Wax Center®XWELL’s first off-airport brand, Naples Wax Center, is a group of upscale hair removal and aesthetic services boutiques. Acquired in mid-September 2023, Naples Wax Center provides core products and service including face and body waxing as well as a range of skincare and cosmetic products from its current three locations.

In December 2024, the Company announced the ongoing expansion of its out-of-airport spas with the opening of a new Naples Wax location in Estero, FL. This opening is the first in a series of strategic growth initiatives to expand the XWELL brand beyond airports. Looking ahead, in addition to its Estero location, XWELL has plans to open 6 additional locations across Florida during 2025.

New York City’s Penn Station XpresSpa®

Consistent with XWELL’s strategy to extend its footprint into transportation hubs, the Company is executing plans to open an XpresSpa location in New York City’s Penn Station in 2025. The tech-forward spa will serve commuters, neighborhood locals, and tourists with wellness-focused retail, autonomous massage, and nail care services, enabling seamless and efficient experiences for time-crunched New York City travelers.

Life Sciences & Biosurveillance — XpresCheck® and HyperPointe™XpresCheck in collaboration with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (“CDC”) and Ginkgo Bioworks, currently operates biosurveillance stations in 8 of the nation’s busiest airports.

In March of 2025, XWELL announced that the CDC extended its Traveler-based Genomic Surveillance Program for three years. The contract has a total base value of $53.7 million over three years, with a maximum ceiling value of $85.7 million within the same timeframe. This program has been supported in whole or in part by the Centers for Disease Control & Prevention under contract number 75D30125C20439.  

The TGS program functions as an early detection platform for emerging pathogens. By providing multimodal data, it enhances global biosecurity and illuminates migratory disease origin, to inform medical countermeasure research and development. The program utilizes wastewater samples from inbound international aircraft and airport triturators, along with nasal swab samples from volunteers arriving in the U.S. on select international flights.

Additionally, the Company began reporting operating results for HyperPointe within its XpresCheck business. Beginning in June 2020, and following its acquisition by XWELL in January 2022, HyperPointe’s management team and suite of services and technology have been utilized to develop and deploy the technological infrastructure necessary to scale the growth of the XpresCheck business. HyperPointe’s experience in this space continues to play a critical role in the expansion of ongoing biosurveillance efforts created in partnership with Ginkgo Bioworks and the CDC.

Liquidity and Financial ConditionAs of December 31, 2024, the Company had approximately $4.6 million of cash and cash equivalents (excluding restricted cash), approximately $7.3 million in marketable securities, total current assets of approximately $15.3 million, and no long-term debt.

The Company significantly reduced operating and overhead expenses in the 2023 and 2024, while it continues to focus on returning to overall profitability.

In January 2025, the Company announced the closing of its private placement offering of $4.0 million of the Company’s newly designated Series G Convertible Preferred Stock. The Company also issued to the investors in the private placement Series A warrants and Series B warrants exercisable for the Company’s common stock. The gross proceeds of the private placement were approximately $4.0 million, before deducting other offering expenses payable by the Company.

Summary 2024 Financial Results

Total RevenueTotal revenue for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 was $33.9 million compared to $30.1 million in the prior year.

Revenue for 2024 primarily consisted of approximately $18.3 million from XpresSpa locations, $430,000 from Treat locations and approximately $13.1 million from XpresTest, which includes XWELL’s bio-surveillance partnership and its HyperPointe business. Naples Wax Center accounted for approximately $2.1 million.

Total Cost of SalesTotal cost of sales for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 were approximately $25.0 million compared to approximately $26.4 million in the prior year.

General and Administrative Expenses; Salaries and BenefitsGeneral and administrative expenses for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 were approximately $12.5 million compared to approximately $13.0 million in the prior year.

Salaries and benefits for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 were approximately $7.5 million compared to approximately $8.0 million in the prior year.

Total Operating ExpensesTotal operating expenses for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 were approximately $25.6 million compared to approximately $31.9 million in the prior year.

Operating LossThe operating loss for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 totaled approximately ($16.7) million compared to approximately ($28.2) million in the prior year.

Net Loss Attributable to XWELLNet loss attributable to XWELL for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 totaled approximately ($16.9) million compared to approximately ($27.7) million in the prior year.

About XWELL, Inc.  

XWELL, Inc. is a leading global wellness holding company operating multiple brands: XWELL™, XpresSpa®, Treat™, Naples Wax Center®, XpresCheck® and HyperPointe™.  

  • XpresSpa is a leading retailer of wellness services and related products.  
  • Naples Wax Center is a group of upscale skin care boutiques.  
  • XpresCheck, in partnership with the CDC and Ginkgo Biosecurity, conducts biosurveillance monitoring in its airport locations.
  • HyperPointe is a leading digital healthcare and data analytics relationship company serving the global healthcare industry.  

For more information on XWELL’s offerings, visit www.XWELL.com. 

Forward-Looking Statements  

This press release may contain "forward-looking" statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. These include statements preceded by, followed by or that otherwise include the words "believes," "expects," "anticipates," "estimates," "projects," "intends," "should," "seeks," "future," "continue," or the negative of such terms, or other comparable terminology. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those indicated by such forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements relating to expectations about future results or events are based upon information available to XWELL as of the date of this press release, and are not guarantees of the future performance of the Company, and actual results may vary materially from the results and expectations discussed. Additional information concerning these and other risks is contained in the Company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K, as amended, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, and other Securities and Exchange Commission filings. All subsequent written and oral forward-looking statements concerning XWELL, or other matters and attributable to XWELL or any person acting on its behalf are expressly qualified in their entirety by the cautionary statements above. XWELL does not undertake any obligation to publicly update any of these forward-looking statements to reflect events or circumstances that may arise after the date hereof.

data.title: Industrial Shares Fall Amid Lower VolatilityIndustrials Roundup
data.description:
Industrial shares fell as Wall Street took a break from the nerve-wrecking swings that marked trading since President Trump announced his tariffs package on April 2.

Boeing shares fell after Beijing told Chinese airlines not to place new orders for Boeing jets and is requiring carriers to seek approval before taking delivery of aircraft they have already ordered, according to people with knowledge of the Chinese regulator's guidance.

Redwire's shares rose after it won a contract to provide docking systems for the European Space Agency's Lunar I-Hab.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp, traditionally known for its ice cream business, has released its latest 10-K report, showcasing a significant transformation into a diversified technology holding company. The report highlights the company's strategic shift, financial performance, and the challenges it faces as it ventures into new markets and technologies.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $4.81 million. The increase in revenue is attributed to the acquisition of RedGear in October 2023 and the asset acquisition of Singer Networks in April 2024.

Gross Profit: $2.98 million. Improved efficiency and reduced costs contributed to a higher gross profit margin.

Net Income: $4.40 million. The net profit was primarily due to a large fair market value change and gain on debt extinguishment.

Basic Income (loss) per share: $0.01. This reflects the company's return to profitability compared to the previous year's loss.

Business Highlights

Business Transformation: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp is transitioning into a diversified technology holding company, inspired by models like Berkshire Hathaway and Alphabet. This shift aims to build a portfolio of high-growth technology subsidiaries.

Core Business Focus: The flagship operating company, ReachOut, has evolved into a national leader in cybersecurity solutions for SMBs, offering services like 24/7 threat monitoring and AI-driven automation through a subscription-based model.

Strategic Evolution: Post-acquisition, ReachOut has shifted its growth strategy to target smaller, high-potential firms for faster integration and scalability, enhancing service offerings and operational efficiency.

New Ventures: The company is expanding its portfolio beyond traditional IT services with ventures like TRUSTLESS, a blockchain-based identity management platform, to diversify revenue streams and capitalize on emerging market opportunities.

Market Positioning: ReachOut aims to emulate a customer-centric approach similar to T-Mobile, providing accessible, high-quality cybersecurity services to underserved SMBs across the nation.

Geographical Performance: The company operates two regional entities, with IND providing technological services from New Jersey and Illinois, generating 62% of consolidated sales, and RedGear operating in Texas and Arizona, generating 38% of sales.

Future Growth Strategy: ReachOut plans to expand as a holding company, establish a nationally recognized brand in cybersecurity and IT, execute high-velocity, high-margin rollups, and launch disruptive platforms like TRUSTLESS.

AI-First Operations: The integration of AI copilots and intelligent automation is a key strategy to reduce headcount pressure while increasing client satisfaction and operational efficiency.

Organic Growth: The company leverages Rick Jordan’s personal brand and media presence to fuel inbound demand and elevate ReachOut’s media presence, driving lead volume and customer conversion.

Compliance-Driven Market Penetration: ReachOut is building industry-specific offerings for regulation-heavy sectors like education, defense, and healthcare, aligning with its security-first, recurring revenue foundation.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp undertook several strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of ReachOut Technology Corp., which was structured as a reverse merger. This acquisition was aimed at expanding the company's capabilities in cybersecurity and IT services. Additionally, the company acquired RedGear LLC and the assets of Singer Networks, LLC to enhance its market presence and service offerings in the technology sector.

Capital Management: The company engaged in various capital management activities, including the issuance of convertible notes and preferred stock to finance operations and acquisitions. It also managed its liabilities through debt extinguishment agreements, resulting in significant gains from the forgiveness of certain debts. The company faced challenges with several notes in default but managed to negotiate waivers and forbearance agreements. Furthermore, the company settled amounts due to officers and affiliates through the issuance of preferred stock.

Future Outlook: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp plans to focus on generating sufficient cash flows from operations to meet its obligations and continue as a going concern. The company aims to reduce expenses and explore financing options through the sale of debt and equity securities to support its strategic initiatives and capital management goals. The company is also considering the potential sale of assets acquired from Singer Networks to optimize its asset portfolio.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp is transitioning into a diversified technology holding company, which involves significant strategic and operational risks. The shift from acquiring larger entities to targeting smaller, high-potential firms presents integration and scalability challenges. The company's focus on AI and automation requires continuous innovation and investment to maintain service relevance in the rapidly evolving technology landscape.

Risk Factors: The transformation into a holding company and the focus on high-growth technology subsidiaries introduce risks related to integration, cultural alignment, and operational scalability. The technology landscape's rapid evolution, particularly in AI and cybersecurity, poses a risk of obsolescence without continuous innovation. Market volatility, macroeconomic fluctuations, and capital availability may impact the pace of acquisitions and expansion.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: The company reported a net profit for the year ended December 31, 2024, primarily due to a large fair market value change and gain on debt extinguishment. However, the increase in compensation and professional expenses indicates rising operational costs. The reliance on acquisitions for growth, as seen with the acquisition of RedGear and Singer Networks, presents risks related to integration and achieving anticipated synergies. The decrease in costs of goods sold due to improved efficiency and automation highlights the importance of maintaining these efficiencies to sustain profitability.

Market Risk Disclosures: The company's strategy to expand through acquisitions and its focus on emerging technologies like blockchain and AI suggest exposure to market risks associated with technological advancements and competitive pressures.

SEC Filing: Yuenglings Ice Cream Corp [ YCRM ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: TriSalus Life Sciences, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
TriSalus Life Sciences, Inc., a company dedicated to transforming outcomes for patients with solid tumors through innovative delivery technology and immunotherapeutic solutions, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive healthcare market.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $29.431 million, a 59.0% increase compared to the previous year, driven by higher TriNav sales volumes.
  • Gross Profit: $25.328 million, up by 59.2%, primarily due to higher sales volume and improved production efficiencies.
  • Loss from Operations: $(36.165) million, an improvement of 33.6% from the previous year, reflecting reduced research and development expenses.
  • Net Loss Available to Common Stockholders: $(30.045) million, improved by 49.4% from the previous year.
  • Net Loss Per Share, Basic and Diluted: $(1.31), reflecting the impact of increased sales and reduced operating expenses.

Business Highlights

  • Innovative Delivery Technology: TriSalus focuses on improving therapeutic delivery and patient outcomes by integrating innovative delivery technology with standard-of-care therapies and investigational immunotherapeutic nelitolimod.
  • Pressure-Enabled Drug Delivery (PEDD): The PEDD method enhances intravascular therapeutic delivery into tumors, increasing the likelihood of tumor response compared to conventional delivery technologies.
  • TriNav Infusion System: The TriNav device, a 510(k) FDA-cleared PEDD device, is used in interventional radiology procedures for liver cancer patients, achieving significant growth in usage and revenue.
  • Pancreatic Retrograde Venous Infusion (PRVI) Device: A separate 510(k) cleared PEDD device for pancreatic tumors, currently in clinical trials, aims to address limitations of arterial infusions in the pancreas.
  • Clinical Trials and Studies: TriSalus is conducting Phase 1 and Phase 1b clinical trials for nelitolimod across various cancer indications, including uveal melanoma, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and pancreatic cancer.
  • Real-world Data and HEOR Study: A Health Economic and Outcome Research study demonstrated that TriNav patients, despite higher disease burden, showed comparable clinical results to those with lower disease burden, highlighting the benefits of PEDD technology.
  • Market Opportunity: The incidence of primary and metastatic liver tumors presents a significant market opportunity for TriNav, with an estimated potential market of approximately 62 thousand units, or $494.0 million.
  • New Product Launches: TriSalus expanded its PEDD device portfolio with the TriNav LV Infusion System and TriGuide Guiding Catheter, targeting larger vessels and optimizing therapeutic delivery.
  • Future Outlook: TriSalus plans to expand its sales organization, develop collaborations with therapeutic partners, and continue partnering with leading academic medical centers to enhance clinical evidence and drive revenue growth.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Shift: TriSalus is transitioning from a company solely selling infusion systems to a therapeutics company, focusing on the acquisition and development of nelitolimod, an immuno-oncology drug, and integrating their PEDD technology with standard-of-care therapies.
  • Product Portfolio Expansion: The company launched the TriNav LV Infusion System and TriGuide Guiding Catheter to target larger vessels, aiming to expand its addressable market.
  • Capital Management: TriSalus entered into a Standby Equity Purchase Agreement (SEPA) with Yorkville, allowing the sale of up to $30 million in common stock, raising $14.1 million so far. Additionally, the company secured a $50 million credit agreement with OrbiMed, drawing $25 million initially and an additional $10 million subsequently. A warrant exchange offering converted a significant portion of outstanding warrants into common stock, and Series A Convertible Preferred Stock issuance raised $40.2 million.
  • Future Outlook: TriSalus plans to continue its transformation into a therapeutics company, focusing on the development and potential FDA approval of nelitolimod. The company anticipates needing additional capital to fund operations and achieve its strategic goals, including potential public and private equity offerings, debt financings, and strategic partnerships. The company aims to maintain adequate reimbursement for its TriNav products and achieve revenue targets to access further funds under the OrbiMed Credit Agreement. However, there is substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern without securing additional financing.

Challenges and Risks

  • Financial Risks: The company faces significant financial risks due to its limited operating history and substantial accumulated losses. It has incurred net losses of $30.0 million and $59.4 million for the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023, respectively, with an accumulated deficit of $279.5 million as of December 31, 2024. The need for additional capital raises substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern, as existing cash and cash equivalents of $8.5 million are insufficient to fund operations for the next 12 months. The company anticipates needing substantial additional funding to finance operations, respond to competitive pressures, and make acquisitions.
  • Operational Risks: The company is highly dependent on its TriNav device for revenue, which poses a risk if market acceptance is not achieved or sustained. Factors such as physician awareness, supply chain disruptions, and reimbursement changes could impact sales. The company also faces operational risks related to its limited marketing, sales, and distribution capabilities, which could hinder revenue growth if not effectively expanded.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are significant, particularly concerning the development and approval of nelitolimod, the company's sole pharmaceutical product candidate. The company is in early clinical development stages and faces uncertainties in obtaining regulatory approvals, which could delay or prevent commercialization. Additionally, changes in reimbursement status for TriNav could materially affect sales and market adoption.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including competition from larger, more established companies with greater resources. This competition could impact the company's ability to establish its products in the marketplace and maintain or grow market share.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges of achieving profitability and sustaining operations without additional capital. The company plans to seek strategic alliances, licensing arrangements, and public or private financings to address its capital needs. Management is also focused on expanding its marketing and sales capabilities to increase TriNav adoption and exploring partnerships to advance nelitolimod development.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: The company is subject to market risks related to interest rates and credit conditions, which could impact its ability to raise funds on favorable terms. Global economic conditions and geopolitical events, such as wars and banking disruptions, may further exacerbate these risks, affecting the company's financial stability and growth prospects.

SEC Filing: TriSalus Life Sciences, Inc. [ TLSI ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: SUNation Energy, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
SUNation Energy, Inc., a leading provider of photovoltaic solar energy systems and batteries for residential homeowners, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report outlines significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, along with strategic initiatives aimed at navigating these hurdles and positioning the company for future growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales: $56.86 million, a decrease of 29% from $79.63 million in 2023, attributed to an overall industry contraction in the residential solar market.
  • Gross Profit: $20.43 million, a decrease of 26% from $27.70 million in 2023, with gross margin increasing to 35.9% from 34.8% in 2023.
  • Operating Loss from Continuing Operations: $12.32 million, an increase of 65% from $7.47 million in 2023, driven by goodwill and intangible asset impairment losses.
  • Net Loss: $15.85 million, an increase of 94.9% from $8.13 million in 2023, reflecting higher operating losses and impairment charges.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(50.58), compared to $(611.55) in 2023, adjusted for reverse stock splits.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: SUNation Energy's revenue is primarily derived from photovoltaic solar energy systems and batteries for residential homeowners, accounting for 82% of 2024 consolidated revenue. The average system size installed was 6.1 kilowatts for HEC customers in Hawaii and 11.4 kilowatts for SUNation’s regional customers on Long Island, New York.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in New York, Florida, and Hawaii. It experienced a 24% decrease in sales in the New York market and a 37% decrease in sales in the Hawaii market in 2024 compared to 2023. The decline in Hawaii was partly due to the end of the Battery Bonus program, which had incentivized energy storage additions.
  • Sales Units: The company saw a 12% reduction in residential kilowatts installed in 2024 compared to 2023. Additionally, there was a 51% decrease in battery capacity installed within HEC, contributing to the overall decline in sales.
  • Future Outlook: SUNation Energy plans to continue its strategy of acquiring, integrating, and growing local and regional solar, storage, and energy services companies. The company aims to leverage its customer-centric approach and strong vendor relationships to enhance its market position. Additionally, SUNation Energy is exploring opportunities to expand its offerings beyond solar to become a one-stop shop for consumers' home and energy needs.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Corporate Treasury Strategy: SUNation Energy has adopted a corporate treasury strategy that includes bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset, aiming to support its planned expansion strategy and operational requirements. The company is also exploring the use of bitcoin as a payment option for customers and suppliers, aligning with its mission to make solar power more accessible.
  • Capital Management: The company executed two reverse stock splits in 2024 to consolidate its shares and improve its capital structure. The June 2024 reverse stock split was at a 1-for-15 ratio, and the October 2024 reverse stock split was at a 1-for-50 ratio. These actions reduced the number of shares outstanding and adjusted the authorized shares accordingly. SUNation Energy also raised capital through a registered direct offering and an at-the-market offering, generating proceeds to support its financial obligations.
  • Future Outlook: SUNation Energy faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to its current financial position and forecasted cash flows. The company plans to raise additional funding through public or private equity offerings, debt financings, and strategic alliances to meet its obligations and support its operations. The company is also focused on reducing its debt and contingent liabilities, having recently entered into a securities purchase agreement to raise $20 million in gross proceeds. This capital will be used to pay off outstanding debt and support future growth initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Nasdaq Listing Risks: The company faces significant risks related to its listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market. A recent non-compliance notice regarding the minimum bid price requirement could lead to delisting, affecting stock liquidity and financing capabilities. The company plans to request a hearing to address this issue, but there is no assurance of a favorable outcome.
  • Shareholder Dilution: The issuance of additional capital stock to raise funds for operations or acquisitions may dilute existing shareholders' ownership and affect earnings per share. The company has authorized a substantial number of shares for potential issuance, which could impact shareholder influence and stock value.
  • Internal Controls: The company has identified material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting, which could lead to inaccurate financial reporting and affect investor confidence. Efforts to remediate these weaknesses are ongoing, but challenges remain due to limited resources.
  • Stock Price Volatility: The volatility in the company's stock price and trading volume poses a risk to shareholder value. Factors such as market conditions, economic changes, and industry developments contribute to this volatility.
  • Growth Strategy Risks: The company's growth strategy is heavily reliant on the continued origination of solar installation agreements. Challenges such as supply chain issues, regulatory changes, and competition could impact the company's ability to secure new agreements and expand its business.
  • Going Concern: The company needs additional capital to fund operations and repay obligations, with substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. This uncertainty may affect stock price and capital-raising efforts.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in the price of solar energy system components and potential increases in tariffs. These factors could affect the cost structure and competitiveness of the company's offerings.
  • Bitcoin Strategy Risks: The company's bitcoin acquisition strategy introduces additional risks, including the volatility of bitcoin prices and potential regulatory challenges. The strategy has not been tested, and any adverse developments could impact financial results and stock performance.

SEC Filing: SUNation Energy, Inc. [ PEGY ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

data.title: FOXO TECHNOLOGIES INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
FOXO Technologies Inc., a company operating in the healthcare and epigenetic diagnostics sectors, has released its annual 10-K report, detailing significant financial improvements, strategic acquisitions, and future growth plans. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the evolving market landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenues: $4.1 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year due to contributions from newly acquired entities Myrtle and RCHI.
  • Loss from Operations: $(7.8) million, showing an improvement from the previous year due to reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Attributable to FOXO: $(12.4) million, a substantial improvement from the prior year's loss, driven by decreased operating losses and absence of certain non-recurring charges.
  • Net Loss to Common Stockholders: $(13.5) million, improved from the previous year's $(29.8) million, primarily due to reduced operating expenses and lower deemed dividends.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: FOXO Technologies Inc. operates in two main segments: Healthcare and Labs and Life. The Healthcare segment includes a rural hospital division and a mental and behavioral health division, while the Labs and Life segment focuses on epigenetic diagnostics and interpretation.
  • Healthcare Segment Expansion: The company expanded its Healthcare segment with the acquisition of Myrtle Recovery Centers, Inc. and Rennova Community Health, Inc. in 2024. Myrtle offers behavioral health services, and Rennova operates a critical access hospital in Tennessee.
  • Behavioral Health Services: Myrtle Recovery Centers, Inc. provides substance use disorder treatments on both inpatient and outpatient bases. The facility in Oneida, Tennessee, began operations in August 2023 and offers detoxification and rehabilitation services.
  • Critical Access Hospital Operations: Rennova Community Health, Inc. operates the Big South Fork Medical Center, a 25-bed hospital with a 24/7 emergency department, providing various ancillary services. The hospital is designated as a Critical Access Hospital.
  • Labs and Life Segment Focus: The Labs and Life segment is commercializing epigenetic biomarker technology for the life insurance industry, focusing on saliva-based health and wellness biomarker solutions for underwriting and risk assessment.
  • Epigenetic Biomarker Technology: FOXO Labs is pioneering the integration of epigenetic biomarkers into underwriting protocols and consumer engagement tools, using machine learning and AI technologies to transform human health and longevity.
  • Future Outlook for Healthcare Segment: The company plans to grow its Healthcare division through acquisitions and investments in new operations, particularly targeting rural hospital properties or suitable premises.
  • Future Outlook for Labs and Life Segment: FOXO intends to leverage its extensive epigenetic data and expertise to build strategic alliances and develop commercialization opportunities for its product and service offerings.
  • Regulatory Compliance: The company is actively monitoring and complying with federal and state regulations affecting its laboratory testing and healthcare operations, including CLIA and HIPAA requirements.
  • Intellectual Property Strategy: FOXO maintains significant trade-secret intellectual property regarding epigenetic biomarker technology and has filed several patent applications to protect its innovations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Expansion: FOXO Technologies Inc. has strategically expanded into the healthcare sector by acquiring Myrtle Recovery Centers, Inc. and Rennova Community Health, Inc. These acquisitions are intended to complement its existing operations in epigenetic biomarker technology and to leverage synergies between healthcare services and its Labs and Life segment.
  • Shift in Strategic Focus: The company is shifting its strategic focus towards direct-to-consumer wellness testing and bioinformatics services, moving away from selling life insurance products.
  • Capital Management: The company implemented a 1-for-10 reverse stock split to regain compliance with NYSE listing requirements. It has engaged in various capital management activities, including private placements and the issuance of promissory notes to raise funds. FOXO has also exchanged debt for preferred stock to manage its liabilities and improve liquidity. The company has been actively managing its capital structure through the issuance of convertible promissory notes and preferred stock to fund operations and strategic acquisitions.
  • Future Outlook: FOXO plans to continue expanding its healthcare operations by acquiring additional facilities and replicating its business model in other rural areas. The company aims to enhance its bioinformatics services and develop new partnerships to advance its epigenetic research. However, FOXO acknowledges the need for additional financing to support its operations and strategic initiatives, as current revenue is insufficient to fund its business through 2025. The company is exploring equity or debt financing options to secure the necessary capital.

Challenges and Risks

  • Indebtedness: The company faces significant challenges due to its substantial indebtedness, with many debt instruments in payment default, which may limit operational and financial flexibility.
  • Management Control: The management's control over corporate activities without the need for stockholder approval could limit minority shareholders' influence and potentially lead to decisions not in their best interest.
  • Profitability: The company has a history of losses and may not achieve profitability, requiring additional capital to fund operations beyond December 2025, which may not be available on favorable terms.
  • Regulatory Risks: The healthcare operations are subject to regulatory risks, including potential changes to the ACA, which could adversely affect operations. The company must comply with complex federal and state laws, and any violations could result in significant penalties.
  • Data Security: The company is exposed to risks related to the security of patient information, which could lead to substantial costs and litigation if breached.
  • Supplier Dependence: The epigenetic testing services face competition and rely on a limited number of critical third-party suppliers, which poses a risk if these suppliers fail to deliver.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could increase the cost of capital. Economic changes could adversely impact the business, particularly in the healthcare and biotechnology sectors. The company also faces risks related to the volatility of the public market for its securities, which could affect its ability to raise additional funds.

SEC Filing: FOXO TECHNOLOGIES INC. [ FOXO ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2025

Ohio Valley Banc Corp. Increases Dividend 4.5%
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Ohio Valley Banc Corp raised its dividend to 23 cents from 22 cents.

The new payout, equal to 92 cents a year, represents an annual yield of about 3.1% based on Tuesday's closing price of $29.65.

The regional bank holding company said the higher dividend will be paid May 10 to shareholders of record as of April 25.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Kroger Names Kelley New Head of Retail Divisions Amid Grocery Leader Changes
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Kroger named a new head of its retail divisions as it appointed new presidents of three of its grocery chains.

The supermarket chain on Tuesday said it appointed Joe Kelley as the senior vice president of retail divisions at a time when the company is working to shuffle its supply chain due to tariffs threats and growing consumer demand for fresh products.

Kelley, who joined Kroger in 2019, succeeds Kenny Kimball, who will be returning to the Utah-based Smith's business as president.

As a result, Kroger named Chris Albi as the president of King Soopers and City Markets.

The company also appointed Kendra Doyel as the president of Food 4 Less to replace Bryan Kaltenbach, who is retiring.

Other Kroger grocery chains beyond its namesake business include Roundy's, Ralphs, Mariano's and Dillons.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com

data.title: United Airlines Bucks Industry Downturn With 1Q Earnings BeatUpdate
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

United Airlines swung to a profit and posted higher revenue in the first quarter despite what the airline is calling a challenging economic environment.

The airline offered two sets of profit guidance depending on whether the U.S. enters a recession or not as it reported quarterly results that beat Wall Street expectations. United also said it would reduce its number of flights and aircraft in anticipation of lower travel demand and a potentially weaker U.S. economy. The reductions come as consumers pull back on leisure travel, leading United and its competitors to lower their views and cut capacity in recent weeks.

"The company's outlook is dependent on the macro environment which the company believes is impossible to predict this year with any degree of confidence," United said in an investor update.

United posted a first-quarter profit of $387 million, or $1.16 a share, in the three months ended March 31, compared with a loss of $124 million, or 38 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were 91 cents, ahead of the 74 cents forecast by analysts, according to FactSet. United had previously said it expected adjusted earnings per share to be on the low end of its guidance range, which was 75 cents to $1.25, due to falling demand.

Revenue rose 5.4% to $13.21 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $13.23 billion. Revenue per available seat mile for domestic passengers fell 4% from the previous year.

The airline on Tuesday shared two sets of guidance for full-year adjusted earnings per share. One was unchanged from its previous estimate of $11.50 to $13.50, while the other was based on a scenario where the U.S. enters a recession, lowered to $7 to $9. In the second quarter, United expects $3.25 a share to $4.25 a share.

Shares rose 6.6% to $71.43 after the market closed Tuesday. Despite the after-hours gains, the stock has lost 38% of its value over the past three months. Stocks for Delta Air Lines and American Airlines also increased in after-hours trading after United reported.

United said it is removing four percentage points of scheduled domestic capacity starting in the third quarter of 2025. It is also making adjustments to the utilization rate of its fleet, including making reductions on lower demand days. That is on top of previously stated plans to retire 21 aircraft early.

Domestic leisure travel demand had started to decline earlier this year, Chief Executive Scott Kirby said in March. There was also a 50% drop in government-related travel, which makes up to 5% of United's business.

"We will continue to execute our multiyear plan that has allowed United to thrive in any demand environment," Chief Executive Scott Kirby said. "It has given us industry-leading margins in the good times and we expect to expand our lead further in challenging economic times."

Delta Air Lines last week withdrew its full-year guidance, citing uncertainty about tariffs. It also said it plans to cap capacity growth in the back half of the year and is not taking any new aircraft deliveries this year. Budget airline Frontier also withdrew its full-year forecast and said it expects revenue growth to be nine percentage points lower than it previously anticipated.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Lithia & Driveway COO Adam Chamberlain to Join Mercedes-Benz USA in June
data.description:
Lithia & Driveway said late Tuesday that Adam Chamberlain will move from his current role as Chief Operating Officer to become the chief executive officer of Mercedes-Benz USA, starting June 1.

The company said its regional presidents and vice presidents will now report directly to its president and CEO, Bryan DeBoer.

data.title: Ostin Technology Prices $5 Million Registered Securities Offering
data.description:
Ostin Technology Group priced a registered offering to sell 9.09 million Class A ordinary shares and related warrants to several investors to raise $5 million.

Each share is being sold together with two associated warrants, each to purchase one share, at a combined offering price of $0.55 apiece, the company said Tuesday in a statement.

Each warrant has an exercise price of $0.80 and a two-year expiration period.

Ostin Technology stock fell 8.8% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Labor board firings would leave Federal Reserve vulnerable, lawyers say
data.description:
President Donald Trump's firing of Democrats from two federal labor boards leaves the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve vulnerable should his actions be allowed to stand, lawyers for the two fired individuals wrote on Tuesday in asking the Supreme Court to reject the Trump administration's case.

Allowing the firing of Cathy Harris from the Merit Systems Protection Board and Gwynne Wilcox from the National Labor Relations Board before their terms expired would have implications for agencies ranging from the National Transportation Safety Board to the Fed by paving the way for Trump to dismiss any of their independent directors at will, lawyers for both Harris and Wilcox wrote in separate briefs to the U.S. high court.

It "would greenlight chaos, including the potential destruction of the independent Federal Reserve on which the national economy relies," Harris' lawyers said.

The Supreme Court last week cleared the way for Trump to remove Harris and Wilcox for now. The stay gives the nine justices time to consider the Trump administration's formal request to block lower courts' orders to reinstate them while litigation over the firings continues.

The case is being closely followed by economists and other Fed watchers given the fractious relationship between Trump and Fed Chair Jerome Powell. Elevated from member of the Board of Governors to central bank head by Trump during his first term in the White House, Powell soon fell out of favor with the Republican president, who repeatedly threatened to try to fire him.

The Fed, created by an act of Congress in 1913, sets U.S. monetary policy independent of the executive branch. The seven-member board is appointed by the president with confirmation by the Senate, as is the Fed chair. But it has long been the operating assumption that a chair or board member could not be removed over political or policy disagreements.

Trump's removal of the Democrats from the two labor boards has raised concerns about the independence the Fed has long enjoyed - and on which rests much of its policymaking credibility.

"Of particular concern, the independence of the Federal Reserve would become uncertain - a situation that would have dire repercussions for the market," the lawyers for Wilcox wrote.

The lawyers for Harris, meanwhile, made more than two dozen references to the Fed and the risks to it as a bedrock of U.S. economic stability in their filing to the court.

"No matter how the Court couches a stay order, granting a stay in this case will empower the President to undermine the Federal Reserve tomorrow - which is precisely why the Court should deny the application," they wrote.

data.title: Communications Services Stocks Fell TuesdayCommunications Services Roundup
data.description:
U.S. stock markets stabilized, but communications services shares fell as the prospect of trade wars looms large.

Netflix shares rose after the company said it aims to achieve a $1 trillion market capitalization and double its revenue by 2030, ambitious goals that show its growing heft as the largest global streamer. Executives are optimistic about the company's growth prospects, despite lingering concerns on Wall Street about the economy and trade-policy uncertainty.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Synaptics to Report Third Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results on May 8, 2025
data.description:
SAN JOSE, Calif., April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Synaptics® Incorporated (Nasdaq: SYNA) today announced that it will report financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 on Thursday, May 8, 2025, after the market closes. The Company will host a corresponding conference call for analysts and investors at 2:00 p.m. PT (5:00 p.m. ET), to discuss the results.

To participate on the live call, analysts and investors should pre-register at Synaptics Q3 FY2025 Earnings Call Registration.

https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI116ee59c921049ac96b9faa761d08c9c

Registrants will receive dial-in information and a unique passcode to access the call. We encourage participants to dial-in into the call ten minutes ahead of scheduled time.

A live and archived webcast of the conference call will be accessible from the “Investor Relations” section of the Company’s website at https://investor.synaptics.com.

About Synaptics Incorporated:

Synaptics (Nasdaq: SYNA) is leading the charge in AI at the Edge, bringing AI closer to end users and transforming how we engage with intelligent connected devices, whether at home, at work, or on the move. As a go-to partner for the world’s most forward-thinking product innovators, Synaptics powers the future with its cutting-edge Synaptics Astra™ AI-Native embedded compute, VerosTM wireless connectivity, and multimodal sensing solutions. We’re making the digital experience smarter, faster, more intuitive, secure, and seamless. From touch, display, and biometrics to AI-driven wireless connectivity, video, vision, audio, speech, and security processing, Synaptics is the force behind the next generation of technology enhancing how we live, work, and play. 

Follow Synaptics on LinkedIn, X, and Facebook, or visit www.synaptics.com.

For further information, please contact:

Munjal Shah

Synaptics

+1-408-518-7639

munjal.shah@synaptics.com
